summary: Ultra‑matrix capture delivers a lively, well‑balanced sound that lets the energetic 9/12/88 performance shine, with Jerry’s heartfelt vocals on “Goodbye Momma & Poppa” and “Wish I Was a Headlight.”
review: The ultra‑matrix source provides a richer, more natural feel than a dry soundboard, preserving the band’s live energy while keeping the mix clear. The show is praised for its infectious vibe – everyone was dancing – and Jerry’s vocals, though occasionally winded, still delivered strong performances on “Goodbye Momma & Poppa” and “Wish I Was a Headlight.”

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed 4‑star review highlights a solid performance and a good ultra‑matrix recording; the balance of show quality (70%) and recording quality (30%) yields an overall rating around 4.2.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Ultra‑Matrix sounds more like the live mix than a dry soundboard; vocals are described as a bit dry with occasional chipmunk effect, but overall clarity and balance are praised.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert kept the crowd moving, with each song feeding into the next; unique acoustics at each venue added to the show’s distinct ‘thumbprint’ feel.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals sounded winded at times but he gave it his all on key songs.
band_member_comments.Keys: Occasional chipmunk‑like vocalizer from Healy noted, suggesting a quirky keyboard/voice effect.
